title: Enforcement of Monetary Claims – How Does It Work?
description: A comprehensive guide to the enforcement of monetary claims conducted by a court bailiff in Poland. Learn about enforcement measures, costs, and creditor rights.
content:
What Is the Enforcement of Monetary Claims?

The enforcement of monetary claims is a legal process conducted by a court bailiff (komornik sądowy) based on an enforcement title. Its purpose is to compulsorily recover money owed to a creditor when the debtor fails to voluntarily satisfy the obligation. It is the final stage of debt recovery in Polish civil law.

Legal Basis

Monetary enforcement proceedings in Poland are governed primarily by:

  • The Code of Civil Procedure (Art. 758–1088 CCP) – the fundamental act regulating enforcement proceedings,
  • The Act on Court Bailiffs of 22 March 2018,
  • The Act on Bailiff Costs of 28 February 2018.

How to Initiate Enforcement?

To initiate enforcement proceedings, the creditor must file an enforcement application at the bailiff's office, together with the original enforcement title (e.g. a court judgment bearing an enforcement clause). The application can be submitted:

  • in person at the office,
  • by registered mail,
  • electronically via the EPU system (Electronic Writ-of-Payment Proceedings).

Enforcement Measures

The bailiff may apply various enforcement measures depending on the debtor's assets:

  • Seizure of wages – the bailiff notifies the debtor's employer to withhold part of the salary (up to 50%, or 60% for maintenance obligations),
  • Seizure of bank accounts – the bank freezes the debtor's funds up to the amount being enforced,
  • Seizure of receivables – attachment of the debtor's claims against third parties,
  • Seizure and sale of movable assets – inventory, valuation and sale at auction or by private treaty,
  • Enforcement from real estate – a complex process involving valuation, public notice and auction.

Locating the Debtor's Assets

The Bailiff's Office No. IX in Otwock uses modern IT tools to identify the debtor's assets, including the CEPIK vehicle registry, OGNIVO bank information exchange system, ZUS PUE social insurance platform, and CEIDG/KRS business registries.

Enforcement Costs

The costs of enforcement proceedings are generally borne by the debtor. The enforcement fee is 10% of the recovered amount. If the debtor voluntarily satisfies the claim after enforcement is initiated, the fee is reduced to 3%.

Duration of Proceedings

The duration depends on the availability of the debtor's assets and the enforcement measures applied. Wage or bank account seizures can be resolved within weeks; real estate enforcement typically takes several months to over a year.

Creditor's Rights

The creditor has the right to access case files (via the Komornik Online system), request additional enforcement actions, and receive regular electronic updates on the progress of the case.

metaDescription: How does the enforcement of monetary claims work in Poland? Enforcement measures, costs and creditor rights. Bailiff's Office No. IX, Harvey Specter, Otwock.
